story.soThat: user'lara permission assignment yapabileyim ve permission'ları yönetebilleyim
story.tasks: - Task 1: Create PermissionsController with endpoints (AC: 3.8.1, 3.8.2) - Task 2: Implement PermissionsService core methods (AC: 3.8.1, 3.8.2, 3.8.3) - Task 3: Implement GET /users/:id/permissions endpoint (AC: 3.8.3) - Task 4: Implement POST /users/:id/permissions (assign) (AC: 3.8.4) - Task 5: Implement DELETE /users/:id/permissions (revoke) (AC: 3.8.5) - Task 6: Create DTOs and Response Models (AC: All) - Task 7: Register module and exports (AC: All) - Task 8: Integration and E2E Testing (AC: All)

criterion.title: GET /permissions endpoint
criterion.description: Admin user with PERMISSIONS.VIEW permission can access. Returns all permissions (PermissionResDto[]). No pagination (permissions are finite, < 100 items). Returns 403 if no permission.

criterion.title: GET /permissions/modules endpoint
criterion.description: Admin user with PERMISSIONS.VIEW permission can access. Returns unique module names (string[]). Example: ['USERS', 'FILES', 'PERMISSIONS']. Returns 403 if no permission.

criterion.title: GET /users/:id/permissions endpoint
criterion.description: Admin user with PERMISSIONS.VIEW permission can access. Returns user's effective permissions (role + direct, deduplicated). domainID check enforced. Returns 404 if user not found. Returns 403 if no permission or wrong domain.

criterion.title: POST /users/:id/permissions endpoint (assign)
criterion.description: Admin user with PERMISSIONS.ASSIGN permission can access. Request body: { permissionIDs: string[] }. Validates: all permissionIDs exist in Permission table. Validates: user exists and in same domain. Bulk creates UserPermission records. Skips duplicates (upsert logic). Wrapped in transaction (all-or-nothing). Returns 200 OK with success message. Returns 400 if invalid permissionIDs, 404 if user not found, 403 if no permission.

criterion.title: DELETE /users/:id/permissions endpoint (revoke)
criterion.description: Admin user with PERMISSIONS.REVOKE permission can access. Request body: { permissionIDs: string[] }. Bulk deletes UserPermission records. domainID check enforced. Wrapped in transaction. Returns 200 OK with success message. Returns 400 if invalid request, 404 if user not found, 403 if no permission.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: User Management & RBAC Permissions
doc.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-3.8.1 through AC-3.8.5). API endpoint specifications for permission management. Permission Assignment Flow diagrams. Services and Modules component responsibilities.

doc.title: Boilerplate - Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Epic-level story breakdown. Five acceptance criteria covering GET /permissions, GET /permissions/modules, GET /users/:id/permissions, POST /users/:id/permissions (assign), DELETE /users/:id/permissions (revoke).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Detailed API specifications with request/response DTOs. Bulk operations for performance. DomainID filtering mandatory. Return combined permissions (role-based + direct).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Service interface with hasPermission() and getUserPermissions() methods. Combined permission query pattern: UserRole → RolePermission UNION UserPermission → Permission.

doc.title: Story 3.7: Permission Sync Script
doc.snippet: Permission sync script completed. 11 permissions synced to database (7 new, 4 existing). All PERMISSIONS constant entries now in Permission table. Script location: scripts/permission-sync.ts.

artifact.reason: Existing authorization service with getUserPermissions() method (Story 3.5). Can be reused for GET /users/:id/permissions endpoint. Provides hasPermission() for guard integration.
Type-safe permission constants. Includes PERMISSIONS.VIEW, PERMISSIONS.ASSIGN, PERMISSIONS.REVOKE needed for Story 3.8. Format: MODULE.ACTION (e.g., 'PERMISSIONS.ASSIGN').
Permissions module definition. Need to update to register PermissionsController and export PermissionsService.
@Permission decorator for route protection. Use for all controller endpoints: @Permission('PERMISSIONS', ActionEnum.VIEW), @Permission('PERMISSIONS', ActionEnum.ASSIGN), etc.
Route guard for permission enforcement. Apply with JwtAuthGuard to controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard).
Prisma database service for querying Permission, UserPermission entities. Use for getAllPermissions(), getPermissionModules(), assignPermissionsToUser(), revokePermissionsFromUser().

constraint.description: Controller-Service Pattern: PermissionsController delegates business logic to PermissionsService. AuthorizationService used for permission checks via PermissionsGuard.

All permission operations enforce domainID filtering. User must exist in same domain as current user (cross-domain permission assignment blocked). UserPermission entity includes domainID field for isolation.

Bulk operations for efficiency. POST /users/:id/permissions uses Prisma createMany with skipDuplicates: true. DELETE /users/:id/permissions uses Prisma deleteMany. Transaction wrapping ensures atomicity.

All endpoints protected with JwtAuthGuard + PermissionsGuard. Permission checks: PERMISSIONS.VIEW for GET endpoints, PERMISSIONS.ASSIGN for POST, PERMISSIONS.REVOKE for DELETE. No cross-domain access allowed.

getUserPermissions combines role-based + direct permissions in single query. Uses Prisma include + distinct for deduplication. Query pattern: UserRole → RolePermission UNION UserPermission → Permission.
DTO validation with class-validator. AssignPermissionsDto: @IsArray(), @IsUUID('4', { each: true }). Validate all permissionIDs exist before bulk insert. Validate user exists and in same domain.

interface.name: GET /users/:id/permissions
interface.signature: @Get('/users/:id/permissions') @Permission('PERMISSIONS', ActionEnum.VIEW) getUserPermissions(@Param('id') userId: string, @DomainID() domainID: string): Promise<PermissionResDto[]>

interface.name: POST /users/:id/permissions
interface.signature: @Post('/users/:id/permissions') @Permission('PERMISSIONS', ActionEnum.ASSIGN) assignPermissions(@Param('id') userId: string, @Body() dto: AssignPermissionsDto, @DomainID() domainID: string): Promise<{ message: string }>

interface.name: DELETE /users/:id/permissions
interface.signature: @Delete('/users/:id/permissions') @Permission('PERMISSIONS', ActionEnum.REVOKE) revokePermissions(@Param('id') userId: string, @Body() dto: AssignPermissionsDto, @DomainID() domainID: string): Promise<{ message: string }>

interface.name: PermissionsService.assignPermissionsToUser
interface.signature: assignPermissionsToUser(userId: string, permissionIDs: string[], domainID: string): Promise<void>

interface.name: PermissionsService.revokePermissionsFromUser
interface.signature: revokePermissionsFromUser(userId: string, permissionIDs: string[], domainID: string): Promise<void>

tests.standards: Integration testing for all API endpoints using Jest + Supertest. Test database with seeded permissions, users, and roles. Unit testing for PermissionsService methods with mocked PrismaService. E2E testing for admin permission management user journeys. Target 80% overall coverage. Follow existing test patterns from Stories 3.5-3.7 (129 tests passing baseline).

ideas.test: GET /permissions returns all permissions from database, verifies 403 if user lacks PERMISSIONS.VIEW
GET /users/:id/permissions returns effective permissions (role + direct, deduplicated), verifies domainID isolation (403 for cross-domain access), verifies 404 for non-existent user

POST /users/:id/permissions bulk assigns permissions successfully, handles duplicates without error (skipDuplicates: true), validates all permissionIDs exist (400 if invalid), validates user exists and in same domain (404/403), verifies transaction rollback on failure
DELETE /users/:id/permissions bulk revokes permissions successfully, verifies domainID check enforced, verifies transaction wrapping, tests 400/404/403 error cases
Admin assigns permissions to user → User gains access to protected endpoint, Admin revokes permissions → User loses access (403 Forbidden)
